reviews.review_msg: Nice, knowledgable, seasoned mentor in entr.  But when it came to teaching, it was another story.  All exams based off his slides - not enough to study off of (needs a txt) b/c there's confusing financial info he skims thru fast (cap tables). Marks HARD and you don't know what he wantsfor assgnmts. Needs rubric or criteria. AVOID if not doing entr.

He's a nice guy, and pretty chill, but very disorganized, and confusing. Nobody has any idea what is going on in the class, I felt that a lot of his test material was not related to lectures. Also no textbook for this class. I am majoring in entrepreneurship so had to take this class.

Anna is a great prof. But the course is really boring, which means most of the things are common sense but we have to pay three time within Innovation Concentration (240, 338…) Wasted of time and money

This is the first time Ron has taught 314 (New Venture Finance) and he basically reads out of the book and writes it on the board. He really doesn't put a lot of effort into the class. However, he's a relaxed and likeable guy.  Exams are moderate-difficult and sometimes he goes through material quite quickly.

Joshua has experience in the industry, however, I find him not very clear in explaining concepts. He asks us questions and engages in discussion for the whole class, however, I find this method hard to follow at times as I don't know which students provided correct or incorrect interpretations. I wasn't able to take a lot away from each class.

Such a great professor, very knowledgeable about his field of entrepreneurship and innovation. Learned so much real-life applicable stuff in just a semester. He also brings guest lecturers and visitors from the real business world to share their experiences with students and suggests quality content podcasts. Highly recommended!
